hero.subtitle: Building High-Quality Data to Power the Future of Intelligence
hero.scrollHint: See Our Expert Data Construction Pipeline

datasets.name: HLE Dataset
datasets.desc: High-difficulty science reasoning benchmark spanning mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology, medicine, CS, engineering, finance, arts, linguistics, astronomy, and geography

datasets.name: SFE Dataset
datasets.desc: Real problems from scientific research scenarios, collected and annotated by domain scientists from actual research work with strong real-world constraints

datasets.name: MicroVQA Dataset
datasets.desc: Specialized multimodal dataset for biological microscopy, featuring visual QA over cell and tissue slice micro-images

datasets.name: Cross-lingual K12QA Dataset
datasets.desc: K12 problem dataset covering 20+ languages, collected by local experts from country-specific textbooks and verified for accuracy

datasets.name: Tri-modal Alignment Dataset
datasets.desc: Video-audio-text tri-modal alignment dataset with frame-level temporal annotations and cross-modal semantic mapping

datasets.name: Proactive Streaming Video
datasets.desc: Streaming video active understanding dataset requiring real-time inference and prediction during video playback

datasets.name: Science Video QA Dataset
datasets.desc: Multimodal QA dataset over science videos (chemistry, biology experiments) covering procedures and phenomenon explanations

datasets.name: Chart Reasoning QA Dataset
datasets.desc: Deep reasoning evaluation data fusing complex charts and natural language, with multi-step derivation and cross-chart-text links

datasets.name: Browser Dataset
datasets.desc: Real user browser operation traces with task decomposition, mid-task decisions, and error recovery paths

datasets.name: Openclaw Dataset
datasets.desc: Cross-platform GUI agent dataset covering desktop and mobile interface operation trajectories

datasets.name: Search Evaluation Dataset
datasets.desc: Multi-hop reasoning search benchmark with diverse query sources spanning multiple information types

expertData.title: Expert Knowledge Is the Foundation of Capability Breakthroughs
expertData.subtitle: High-quality expert annotations outperform crowd-sourced data in training efficiency, reasoning depth, and domain precision

pipeline.heading: Human-AI Collaborative Annotation Pipeline
pipeline.rejectDesc: Discarded if difficulty threshold not met

papers.title: AutoQG: An Automated Framework for Question Generation from Scientific Papers
GPQA: A Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A Benchmark
MMLU-Pro: A More Robust and Challenging MultiTask Language Understanding Benchmark
SCBench: A KV Cache-Centric Benchmark for Long Context

detailedSteps.title: Paper Preprocessing & Structured Parsing
detailedSteps.desc: Frontier LLM performs section-aware paper decomposition, splitting abstract, methods, experiments, and conclusions, outputting structured summaries, core concept lists, and key entity-relation candidate sets

detailedSteps.title: Domain Expert Deep Reading & Understanding
detailedSteps.desc: Matching perfectly aligned domain experts (PhD-level+) from expert pool, focusing on methodological details, implicit assumptions, academic lineage, and knowledge boundaries in the paper

detailedSteps.title: Collaborative Knowledge Graph Construction
detailedSteps.desc: AI extracts entity-relation-entity triples using ontology-guided + section-aware prompting from AutoQG paper. Human experts review, supplement omissions, and distinguish explicit vs implicit association graphs requiring domain knowledge

detailedSteps.title: High-Discriminative Question Design via Reasoning Chains
detailedSteps.desc: Based on multi-hop reasoning paths in knowledge graphs combined with expert domain knowledge, design high-discriminative questions with complex reasoning chains near disciplinary boundaries

detailedSteps.title: AI Difficulty Calibration & Consistency Verification
detailedSteps.desc: Frontier models cross-verify question difficulty: send questions all models can answer back for revision, send questions all models get wrong for human verification of answer correctness

detailedSteps.title: Multi-expert Cross-Blind Review
detailedSteps.desc: Double-blind review protocol: each question scored by at least three independent experts across relevance, answerability, distractor quality, fluency, reasoning depth, evidence traceability, with automatic reliability testing

detailedSteps.title: Final Decision & Data Ingestion
detailedSteps.desc: Based on disciplinary balance, difficulty gradient, question type, and other concerns, senior experts make final decision on ingestion

pipeline.quote: Ten million mediocre conversations can't compare with a thousand corner cases polished by top experts

ai4ss.title: AI4SS4AI
ai4ss.subtitle: Understand human diversity through Social Science. Let AI genuinely give back to the Real World.
ai4ss.loopDesc: Starting from the real world, empowered by the mutual reinforcement of Social Science and AI, and ultimately returning to improve the real world

personaCallout.label: Persona × Skills Combinations
personaCallout.desc: A million-scale multi-dimensional personality and skills taxonomy spanning value systems, cognitive styles, professional competencies, and cultural backgrounds — the foundation of unprecedented data diversity

steps.title: Real-World Observation
steps.desc: Collect first-hand data from real social behaviors, cultural differences, and human interactions — preserving the full complexity of human nature

steps.title: Social Science Extraction
steps.desc: Distill personality traits and behavioral patterns using established frameworks from psychology and sociology, giving data scientific interpretability

steps.title: Personality Diversity Modeling
steps.desc: Build a personality atlas spanning diverse value systems, cognitive styles, and cultural backgrounds — enabling unprecedented data diversity

steps.title: High-Quality Data Generation
steps.desc: Driven by the personality atlas, generate large-scale training data that is simultaneously diverse, authentic, and internally consistent

steps.title: AGI Empowers Reality
steps.desc: AI that truly understands human diversity — making wiser, more empathetic decisions in education, healthcare, and governance

hle.name: HLE
hle.desc: High-level multimodal expert reasoning dataset built by a Tsinghua University expert team of ~60 active masters/PhD students through a human-in-the-loop system. Features high-quality reasoning chains with MAS multi-layered quality verification.
hle.brief: HLE (Hard-Level Evaluation) is a high-difficulty scientific reasoning benchmark for frontier LLMs. Questions are designed and reviewed by domain experts across 12+ disciplines, requiring deep reasoning rather than surface matching. Each question includes complete reasoning chain annotations and difficulty ratings.
hle.structure:
{
  "query": "Under standard conditions, which reaction has the most negative ΔG°?",
  "rubric": "Must provide correct answer with thermodynamic derivation",
  "answer": "Option C, reasoning: ...",
  "domain": "chemistry",
  "difficulty": 4
}

paper.title: GPQA: A Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A Benchmark

sfe.name: SFE
sfe.desc: SFE benchmark for evaluating MLLM scientific cognition from perception to reasoning. Data created by professors (associate level and above) and senior grad students from Tsinghua and other top-985 universities based on their research experience, covering 5 disciplines (astronomy, chemistry, earth science, life science, materials science).
sfe.brief: SFE (Scientific Frontiers Evaluation) extracts questions from real research scenarios, annotated by active researchers. Unlike synthetic data, these questions feature genuine constraints, uncertainties, and cutting-edge challenges that directly test a model's research assistance capabilities.
sfe.structure:
{
  "query": "How to quantify off-target rate after in vivo CRISPR-Cas9 editing?",
  "context": "Based on mouse liver AAV delivery experiment",
  "rubric": "Must cite at least two detection methods and compare sensitivity",
  "answer": "Expert reasoning and conclusion...",
  "field": "molecular_biology"
}

microvqa.name: MicroVQA
microvqa.desc: Microscope visual question answering dataset for scientific discovery, focusing on microscopic images and cellular structures with high difficulty, designed to enhance model capabilities in scientific image understanding.
microvqa.brief: MicroVQA is a visual Q&A dataset specialized for biological microscopy imaging, covering cytology, histology, and pathology. Each sample includes high-resolution microscopy images with multi-turn Q&A pairs annotated by biologists, requiring micrograph understanding and professional terminology reasoning.

crosslingual-k12qa.name: Cross-lingual K12QA
crosslingual-k12qa.desc: K12 exercise dataset covering 20+ languages, collected and verified by native education experts from country-specific textbooks, preserving original problem logic.
crosslingual-k12qa.brief: A K12 education exercise dataset spanning 20+ languages worldwide. Questions are collected by native-speaking education experts from official textbooks and exercise books in each country, multi-round verified to preserve original logic and cultural context. An ideal benchmark for evaluating cross-lingual education capabilities.
crosslingual-k12qa.structure:
{
  "question": "If f(x)=x³-3x+1, find the maximum on [-2,2]",
  "options": ["A. -1", "B. 1", "C. 3", "D. 5"],
  "answer": "C",
  "explanation": "f'(x)=3x²-3, set f'(x)=0...",
  "language": "en",
  "grade": 11,
  "subject": "math"
}

superchem-extension.name: SuperChem-Extension
superchem-extension.desc: Deep chemistry domain extension dataset covering high-difficulty chemistry reasoning problems, built by domain experts to directionally enhance model capabilities in chemistry.
superchem-extension.brief: SuperChem-Extension is a deep chemistry domain extension of the SuperChem benchmark, maintaining the same distribution. Fields include uuid, field, question_type, bilingual question text (question_en/question_zh) and options (options_en/options_zh), answers (answer_en/answer_zh), full explanations (explanation_en/explanation_zh), with multimodal support. Covers organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry subfields with high-difficulty problems.
superchem-extension.structure:
{
  "uuid": "superchem-ext-0001",
  "field": "organic_chemistry",
  "question_type": "multiple_choice",
  "question_en": "Which of the following is the major product of the Diels-Alder reaction between butadiene and maleic anhydride?",
  "question_zh": "以下哪个是丁二烯与马来酸酐发生狄尔斯-阿尔德反应的主要产物？",
  "question_images": {},
  "options_en": {"A": "4-cyclohexene-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride", "B": "cyclohexane-1,2-dicarboxylic acid", "C": "3-cyclohexene-1,2-diol", "D": "bicyclo[2.2.1]hept-5-ene-2,3-dicarboxylic anhydride"},
  "options_zh": {"A": "4-环己烯-1,2-二甲酸酐", "B": "环己烷-1,2-二甲酸", "C": "3-环己烯-1,2-二醇", "D": "双环[2.2.1]庚-5-烯-2,3-二甲酸酐"},
  "options_images": {},
  "answer_en": ["A"],
  "answer_zh": ["A"],
  "explanation_en": "The Diels-Alder reaction between butadiene (diene) and maleic anhydride (dienophile) yields 4-cyclohexene-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride via a concerted [4+2] cycloaddition.",
  "explanation_zh": "丁二烯（二烯体）与马来酸酐（亲二烯体）通过协同的[4+2]环加成反应，生成4-环己烯-1,2-二甲酸酐。",
  "explanation_images": {}
}

crosslingual-k12-stem.name: Cross-lingual K12 STEM Dataset
crosslingual-k12-stem.desc: Multilingual K12 STEM dataset covering math, physics, chemistry, and biology, supporting cross-lingual subject reasoning capabilities in educational scenarios.
crosslingual-k12-stem.sample: Entropy Multilingual K12 STEM QA Sample 2026.04.02
crosslingual-k12-stem.brief: A multilingual K12 STEM Q&A dataset covering mathematics, physics, chemistry, and biology in Vietnamese, Indonesian, Malay, Thai, and more. Each entry includes subject classification, difficulty level (Easy/Medium/Hard), question text, answer, and optional image. Questions are collected by native education experts from official textbooks, preserving original problem logic and cultural context.
crosslingual-k12-stem.structure:
{
  "language": "vi",
  "subject": "Mathematic",
  "difficulty": "Hard",
  "question": "Hình chữ nhật ABCD có cạnh AD = 2 cm. Hình tròn tâm D, bán kính DA và hình tròn tâm C bán kính CB có vị trí như hình vẽ. Hãy tính cạnh CD biết rằng diện tích phần AEB bằng diện tích phần MEN.",
  "answer": "3.14 cm",
  "image": ""
}

multimodal-av.name: Multimodal (Video-Audio Alignment) Data
multimodal-av.desc: Expert-built specialized audio-visual description, analysis, and alignment dataset. Every segment, description, and QA pair is constructed by experts in Entropy's proprietary HITL system with guaranteed quality.
multimodal-av.brief: A tri-modal alignment dataset for multimodal models, providing frame-level alignment annotations across video, audio, and text signals. Suitable for training cross-modal retrieval, multimodal understanding, and generation tasks across diverse scenarios including education, news, and science popularization.

proactive-video.name: Proactive Streaming Video Data
proactive-video.desc: Evaluates models' ability to respond at appropriate moments during streaming video input. Entirely expert-built (pure human effort, models incapable of construction), multi-round proofread, covering 15s-30min durations with diverse scenes and tasks — the absolute frontier of proactive model capabilities.
proactive-video.brief: Challenges models to proactively understand and predict during streaming video playback. Models must make predictions before the video completes, then verify or revise as subsequent frames arrive. Simulates the cognitive process of active reasoning during video viewing.

stem-video.name: STEM Professional Video Reasoning
stem-video.desc: Expert-built STEM discipline video understanding dataset, evaluating deep reasoning and knowledge association capabilities on STEM education and experiment video content.
stem-video.brief: A Q&A dataset specialized for science experiment videos, covering chemistry experiments, biological procedures, and physics demonstrations. Each video includes step decomposition, phenomenon explanations, and safety annotations for evaluating deep understanding of scientific processes.

infograph.name: InfoGraph
infograph.desc: Expert-built infographic understanding dataset covering charts, flowcharts, statistical graphs and more, evaluating the interpretation and reasoning capabilities on complex visual information.
infograph.brief: A deep reasoning dataset for complex chart understanding. Each sample contains one or more related charts with questions requiring multi-step inference. Covers bar charts, line graphs, pie charts, scatter plots, heatmaps, and more.

crosslingual-multimodal.name: Cross-lingual Multimodal Multi-task Evaluation Dataset
crosslingual-multimodal.desc: Comprehensive evaluation dataset covering multilingual and multimodal inputs with diverse task types, for systematically assessing model cross-lingual multimodal understanding and generalization.
crosslingual-multimodal.brief: A comprehensive evaluation dataset covering multilingual and multimodal inputs across 11 task categories including Cultural & Multilingual Understanding, Perception & Classification, General VQA, Visual Reasoning, and Scientific & Mathematical Reasoning. Each entry includes task_id, query language, difficulty level, question type (short_answer/multiple_choice), question text, answer, and optional image. Languages span Hungarian, French, Arabic, and other low-resource languages.
crosslingual-multimodal.structure:
{
  "id": "610892865299",
  "task_category": "Cultural & Multilingual Understanding",
  "task_id": "low_resource_language_eval",
  "query_language": "hu",
  "difficulty": "easy",
  "question_type": "short_answer",
  "question": "Milyen virág óriási képe látható a bal oldali épület falára vetítve?",
  "answer": "Napraforgó",
  "image": "ID_6F88BA50FF574374B57CBFBAD354653F"
}

browser.name: Browser Dataset
browser.desc: Real user browser operation trajectories with task decomposition, mid-task decisions, and error recovery paths
browser.brief: Trajectory dataset collected from real user browser operations, covering e-commerce shopping, information retrieval, form filling, content creation, and more. Each trajectory includes complete action sequences, DOM snapshots, and user intent annotations.

openclaw.name: OpenClaw Trajectory Data
openclaw.desc: Records OpenClaw operational trajectories in structured tasks, including complete workspaces and sessions. Used for training and evaluating model planning, execution, and error correction capabilities for Agent development.
openclaw.brief: A cross-platform GUI control agent training dataset covering Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. Each trajectory includes screenshot sequences, action events, UI element localization, and task goal descriptions.

search-retrieval.name: Information Retrieval Data
search-retrieval.desc: Long-chain information reasoning and retrieval data built in two forms: expert-collected from multi-hop (>5 hops) internet corners, and expert-distilled from real user fuzzy search needs ensuring diversity.
search-retrieval.brief: A multi-hop reasoning evaluation dataset for search-augmented generation (RAG) systems. Each question requires retrieving and synthesizing multiple information sources, covering fact-checking, comparative analysis, causal reasoning, and more.
search-retrieval.structure:
{
  "query": "How effective are COVID vaccines against Omicron variant?",
  "reasoning_type": "comparative",
  "required_sources": 3,
  "evidence_urls": [
    "https://doi.org/10.1038/...",
    "https://www.thelancet.com/..."
  ],
  "answer": "mRNA vaccines show reduced neutralizing titers...",
  "evaluation_rubric": "Must compare at least two vaccine types"
}

oj-competition.name: OJ Competition Problems
oj-competition.desc: High-quality competition problems where seed-2.0-pro pass@2 = 0, supplemented with expert-built testcases (~1,000 per problem). Comprehensively evaluates problem difficulty with updated submissions and multi-level expert-supplemented reference code.
oj-competition.brief: This dataset contains competition problem submissions from an Online Judge system, covering C++17 and PyPy3. Each record includes complete source code, evaluation status (AC/WA/CE/RE), runtime (ms), memory usage (MB), and rejudge results against expert-supplied test cases. All problems achieve seed-2.0-pro pass@2 = 0, making this dataset ideal for evaluating code generation and competition-level programming capabilities.

game-interaction.name: Game Screen Interaction Data
game-interaction.desc: Game interface operation and interaction data covering multiple game scenarios for screen understanding and action decision-making, enhancing model capabilities in GUI perception and game Agent scenarios.
game-interaction.brief: Game screen interaction and operation data covering diverse gaming scenarios for screen understanding and action decision-making. Each recording includes game video (Video), frame-level action details (Action+Camera JSON), and game metadata (System Info). Frames capture camera position/rotation (Euler angles and quaternions), camera intrinsics (fx/fy/cx/cy), player world coordinates, mouse absolute/relative displacement, and keyboard events. Designed for training interactive world models and enhancing GUI perception and game Agent capabilities.
